title: common : inhibit lazy grammar sampler while reasoning is active
body:
## Overview

Inhibit lazy grammar sampler while reasoning is active to avoid grammar constraining when a tool call tag (e.g. `<tool_call>`) is present in the reasoning output.

This PR exposes the reasoning budget sampler state to `common_sampler` where it is aware of both the reasoning budget and grammar samplers. When the reasoning sampler is active, and the grammar is lazy, tokens are not passed to the grammar to avoid triggering within the reasoning block.

For models that are supported, i.e. have a defined end thinking tag, the reasoning budget sampler is always added with a default of `INT_MAX`.

Test changed to run apply before accept, as it is done in practice. Adjusted offset to accommodate. Moved `force_pos++` in `accept()`, which should be what updates the sampler's state. The init function will handle the case when `state == COUNTING && budget <= 0`.

## Additional information

Alternative approach to #20844.

# Requirements

- I have read and agree with the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
- AI usage disclosure: Used to explore a PoC (https://github.com/aldehir/llama.cpp/pull/9). Rewritten by hand, although I took a few things verbatim. Used to review and assist in addressing the discrepancy in the tests.
